feature.title: Generated Trophy Room medal sheet
feature.purpose: Upgrade Trophy Room collectibles from CSS-only medals to a generated game-UI medal spritesheet while preserving lane identity badges and tier filtering.

research.title: Dribbble: Medal game designs
research.takeaway: Game medal inspiration commonly uses polished rank badges, metallic materials, and clear trophy silhouettes.

research.title: Dribbble: Bronze, silver, gold, platinum designs
research.takeaway: Tiered visual systems need material differences that remain recognizable at small UI sizes.

research.title: Shutterstock: Game UI stars trophy illustrations
research.takeaway: Achievement visuals often combine trophies, badges, and high-contrast game UI framing.

research.title: Dreamstime: Medal game UI illustrations
research.takeaway: Game UI medal sets commonly group tier variants into reusable icon families.

imageGeneration.prompt: Create a premium 2x2 spritesheet of four collectible achievement medals for a dark futuristic agent-company dashboard. The medals represent Mythic, Gold, Silver, and Bronze tiers, in that order from top-left to bottom-right. Use polished high-end game UI icon art, crisp 3D-rendered medals, no text, no numbers, no logos, no watermark.

interactions: Trophy cards now render a tier-specific medal sprite from the generated 2x2 sheet.
The lane avatar remains as a small overlay badge on each trophy so source-lane identity is still visible.
Mythic, gold, silver, and bronze map to top-left, top-right, bottom-left, and bottom-right sheet quadrants.
The static asset is versioned in the project under web/assets/trophies and documented in the README.

nextIdeas: Generate separate transparent medal cutouts if the Trophy Room needs larger hero trophies.
Add tier-specific hover particles that inherit the medal material color.
Generate lane-specific trophy frames for minigame-specific outcome types.
Add a trophy icon manifest if future repos want to swap sheets without CSS edits.
